title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. litardierei
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. litardierei, also known as the alpine lady's mantle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of alpine lady's mantle include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Alpine lady's mantle can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in traditional medicine and herbalism.
